claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Neutral/measured risk regime: builder not macro tourist, structural long-horizon conviction, constructive but won't call it cleanly risk-on, focused on shipping through the cycle
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Sharp reversal to regulatory OPTIMISM after the 2024 US election — 'There has never been a better time to start a project in crypto... the regulatory environment has changed completely. This is it.' (Jan 8), 'We are not building a Pause button. We're building a $500b GO button' (Jan 21), 'This couldn't have happened under the Biden administration' — the predicted measured/concerned regulatory stance did not anticipate this pivot from drag to tailwind.

Built and championed a formal 7-category token taxonomy (network tokens as 'the king' vs company-backed, memecoins, arcade, collectible, asset-backed, security) — and took analytical token-level positions (ETH/SOL/UNI as exemplary network tokens; BTC explicitly NOT a memecoin) despite the prediction's 'reasons in sectors not tokens / won't name assets' framing.

New specific interest in non-fiat algorithmic stablecoins ('Is RAI still the only... algorithmic stablecoin?... I expect more experiments here, no LUNA please'), a sharper sub-stance within the stablecoin thesis.

Heavy personal emphasis on proof-of-personhood / Sybil-resistance / 'uniqueness' and AI agents custodying their own wallets and operating autonomously onchain — a specific sharpening of the AI×crypto thesis.
Concrete shipping action: Helios multichain light client for L2 interop and trust-minimized sequencer/bridge verification — operationalizing both the scaling and anti-centralization views.
notes: Nearly every predicted stance is confirmed, frequently via direct personal statements: ZK proving (Jolt/zkVM tweets, Rust ZK hiring, Helios), AI×crypto (agents paying for compute, proof-of-personhood Big Idea + Dec 20 podcast, machines respecting cryptographic constraints), anti-memecoin (the network-token-vs-memecoin distinction work, token taxonomy calling memecoins zero-sum speculation), and infra centralization (Helios targeting sequencer/bridge trust; 'no single source of control' Mar 6). Stablecoins confirmed both via his own Feb 26 'increasingly obvious product-market fit' and the a16z State of Crypto report he co-authored/presents. L2/cheap-blockspace confirmed via Helios-for-L2s, throughput/latency/UX tweets, and the report. DePIN is the weakest confirmation — present in the State of Crypto report and the Dec 20 Big Ideas podcast he participated in, but with no sharp standalone personal quote in the window; labeled confirmed on the strength of his central role in that firm content, none of which contradicts it. Risk regime: his 'builder not macro tourist / structural / shipping through the cycle' disposition held firmly (no tactical macro calls; CSX accelerator push), but his tone warmed from 'measured/neutral' to outright enthusiasm as the regulatory overhang he cited resolved — captured in missed_changes. Token arrays were empty and he avoided outright shilling, consistent with the prediction, though he did stake out analytical positions on specific assets.
